slide_1_bullets.label: Adult Clinic -12.4%
slide_1_bullets.sub: Sole driver of the net decline

slide_1_bullets.label: July & August -34
slide_1_bullets.sub: Zero calendar cover -- purely organic losses

slide_1_bullets.label: Youth Clinic +17.2%
slide_1_bullets.sub: Only type growing -- a genuine bright spot

slide_2_narrative: The overall -12 is a modest headline but the composition tells the real story. Adult Clinic is down 12 events (-12.4%). The genuine growth story: Youth Clinic is up 5 events (+17.2%).
slide_3_narrative: Aug (-18) and Jul (-16) are the two biggest problem months. Meanwhile Oct (+11) and Jun (+10) are the standout performers. The pattern suggests summer peak season demand is softening while shoulder months hold or grow.
slide_4_narrative: The calendar is not the explanation for July and August -- both had flat weekend-day counts. The summer declines (combined -34) are fully organic. No alibi.
slide_5_narrative: Adult Clinic is contracting structurally (-12.4% organic). Adult Race and Youth Race are near-flat organically -- the race product is holding. Youth Clinic is growing organically at +17.2%.
slide_6_narrative: Yes -- we really did lose events. 295 of 1,178 2025 events (25%) did not return in any form. The portfolio added 266 genuinely new events plus 30 recovered from cancellations -- 100% gross replacement. Replacement is weakest in the peak summer months, which is where the declines matter most.
slide_7_narrative: The application pipeline is running ahead of last year's pace through May. The single highest-ROI action is Adult Clinic outreach (May-Aug) -- early signals are flat, not falling, but the late-year spontaneous apps that filled 2025 have not materialized yet. Youth Clinic and Adult Race pipelines are healthy and self-sustaining.
slide_8_narrative: July and August each lose around 50 events but replace roughly 94-93% of them organically -- the ecosystem is self-replenishing but not fast enough to fully offset churn. The net decline is a volume problem, not a demand collapse. The immediate action is August win-back outreach. The strategic action is diagnosing July's churn before Q4 planning.

notes.slide_1:
SLIDE 1 -- Title Overview

Years: 2025 vs 2026  |  Total events: 1,178 vs 1,166  |  Net: -12

By type:
- Adult Race: 823->822 (-1, -0.1%)
- Youth Race: 229->225 (-4, -1.7%)
- Adult Clinic: 97->85 (-12, -12.4%)
- Youth Clinic: 29->34 (+5, 17.2%)

Key theme: Adult Clinic accounts for the full net decline. Youth Clinic is the growth story.
notes.slide_2:
SLIDE 2 -- Event Counts by Type

Key message: The -12 net decline is entirely explained by Adult Clinic.

By type:
- Adult Race: 823->822 (-1, -0.1%)
- Youth Race: 229->225 (-4, -1.7%)
- Adult Clinic: 97->85 (-12, -12.4%)
- Youth Clinic: 29->34 (+5, 17.2%)

Talking point: If you exclude Adult Clinic, the rest of the portfolio is flat.
notes.slide_3:
SLIDE 3 -- Monthly Breakdown

Worst months: Aug (-18), Jul (-16), Feb (-9)
Best months:  Oct (+11), Jun (+10), Mar (+8)

Summer total: -34  |  Calendar cover: zero
notes.slide_4:
SLIDE 4 -- Calendar Impact

July: cal expected 0.0, actual -16, organic -16
August: cal expected 0.0, actual -18, organic -18

No calendar alibi for summer declines -- fully organic.
notes.slide_5:
SLIDE 5 -- Organic Performance

After stripping calendar noise:
- Adult Race: organic -0.1%
- Youth Race: organic -1.7%
- Adult Clinic: organic -12.4%
- Youth Clinic: organic 17.2%

Structural decliners: Adult Clinic
Structural growers: Youth Clinic
notes.slide_6:
SLIDE 6 -- Event Disposition

Segments (of 1,178 2025 events):
- Retained: 746 (63%)
- Shifted: 124
- Lost (truly lost): 295 (25%)
- New in 2026: 266
- Recovered: 30

Replacement rate: 100%
notes.slide_7:
SLIDE 7 -- Application Pipeline

In-year applications through May are running ahead of last year.
Highest priority: Adult Clinic (early pipeline flat, late-year spontaneous apps not materializing).
Second: Adult Race (already +34% ahead in-year).
Third: Youth Clinic (fast-track approvals to maintain momentum).
notes.slide_8:
SLIDE 8 -- Win-Back Opportunity

July (49 lost): Too late for 2026. Q3 outreach to understand WHY they left -- diagnose the churn cycle.
August (55 lost): 10-12 weeks away -- some still recruitable. Target by end of May.

Replacement rate: July 94%, August 93%. Net decline is a VOLUME problem, not a demand collapse.

slide_1_data_note: Data as of May 17, 2026  |  Excl. Cancelled / Declined / Deleted  |  ~85-90% event-level match confidence
slide_3_header: Monthly Breakdown -- Jul & Aug Drive the Declines
slide_3_callout_left: Jul and Aug are driven by races, not clinics. Clinic mix is flat or zero in both months.
slide_3_callout_right: Oct (+11) and Jun (+10) are the two strongest months. Both show genuine organic strength plus new event additions.
slide_4_header: Is This a Calendar Effect?  No -- Not for Jul or Aug
slide_4_alert: Jul and Aug had ZERO change in weekend days (Sat or Sun) between 2025 and 2026. There is no calendar explanation for the declines -- they are fully organic.

slide_4_table_rows.interpretation: Calendar gifted it -- organic demand moved opposite direction
Calendar penalised it -- organic demand moved opposite direction
Zero calendar cover -- full decline is organic
Calendar and organic moving against each other

slide_5_callout_left:
Adult Clinic: -12.4% organic
Structurally declining -- the sole driver of the net -12. Calendar noise cannot explain it.
slide_5_callout_right:
Youth Clinic: +17.2% organic
Only type genuinely growing -- no calendar help. Other races near-flat after stripping noise.
slide_8_header: Jul & Aug: Organic Churn and the Win-Back Opportunity
slide_8_subtitle: Replacement is near-complete -- the net declines come from month-shifting, not failed attrition recovery. But the churn volume itself is the problem:
excel_slack_bullets: 1,166 events in 2026 vs 1,178 in 2025 (-12, -1.0%). Adult Clinic accounts for the full net decline (-12, organic -10.3%); adult race roughly flat; Youth Clinic the only growth story (+19.4% organic).

Summer declines concentrated in races: July -16 (both types ~8 each), August -18 (worst month). Calendar provides zero alibi -- July and August had identical weekend-day counts both years. May is most misleading: +3 raw but organic -13.

Of 1,178 2025 active events: 295 truly lost (did not return); 13 tried to return but were cancelled in the next year -- actionable. 30 recovered from prior cancellations. Aug and Jul had the worst replacement rates.
Oct is the standout: lost a Sunday (calendar headwind -23) but delivered organic +33.2 growth -- strongest month. 266 genuinely brand-new events joined. Jun gain (+10) driven by organic demand.

excel_month_narratives.Jun: Strong organic growth. No calendar tailwind.
excel_month_narratives.Jul: Worst organic month. No calendar explanation -- fully organic attrition.
excel_month_narratives.Aug: Worst organic month. No calendar explanation -- fully organic attrition.
excel_month_narratives.Sep: Modest organic decline. Calendar expected +8. Watch item.
excel_month_narratives.Nov: Significant organic decline. Zero calendar cover -- fully organic.
excel_month_narratives.Dec: Roughly flat organically. No calendar noise.

excel_type_insights.Adult Race: Organic +0.2%. Stable -- small numbers, monitor trend.
excel_type_insights.Youth Race: Organic -1.2%. Stable -- small numbers, monitor trend.
excel_type_insights.Adult Clinic: Structural contraction -10.3%. Key concern -- declining after removing all calendar effects.

excel_calendar_findings: ΔWknd=0 both months. Zero calendar explanation. Entire decline is organic attrition.
Gains +16 expected from calendar. Actual +3 → organic -13. May underperformed its calendar opportunity.
Loses 23 expected from calendar headwind. Actual +10 → organic +33. Strong organic growth overcame the headwind -- standout month.
Saturday count changes (±1). Calendar explains a portion of those months' variance; residual reflects true organic change.

excel_pipeline_findings.Adult Race: Prior-year Q4 apps trending slightly down but in-year applications are running ahead. Organizers are applying LATER, not less. No structural demand concern for races.
excel_pipeline_findings.Youth Race: Pipeline closely tracks active event count. Application flow is healthy. Holding steady.
excel_pipeline_findings.Adult Clinic: LARGEST GAP. Early pipeline identical to prior year but all the advantage came from late-year spontaneous apps (May-Dec) that haven't materialized yet. Proactive outreach May-Aug could recover events and close most of the net decline.
excel_pipeline_findings.Youth Clinic: Prior-year Q4 applications significantly ahead. In-year applications also well ahead. Both early and in-year pipelines expanding. Growth is structural and front-loaded.
excel_pipeline_findings.Overall Pipeline: Total in-year applications are running ahead of prior year pace through May. Year-end active event count will likely be higher than the May snapshot suggests.
